t_ch: 哼，算了，不和你开玩笑了。我就是尤利安，{M#大哥哥}{F#大姐姐}没认错。找我有什么事嘛？
t_en: Hmm... Fine. I'll stop messing with you. You are right, I'm Julian. What do you want from me?

t_ch: 我想问你…
t_en: I'm wondering...

t_ch: 你在这里做什么？
t_en: What are you doing here?

t_ch: 哼哼…{M#大哥哥}{F#大姐姐}果然不明白。
t_en: Hmmm... you seriously do not understand.

t_ch: 我，堂堂「千面尤利安」——正在磨炼自己的伪装技巧呢！
t_en: I, Julian of a Thousand Faces, am honing my own disguise skills!

t_ch: 可你已经很厉害了。
t_en: But you're very skilled already, aren't you?

t_ch: 哎呀，就、就算你这么夸我，我也不会感到开心的哦……
t_en: Well... um... your praise doesn't excite me...

t_ch: 你在修炼魔法？
t_en: Are you practicing magic?

t_ch: 「魔法」？怎么可能，那种东西根本不存在的啦。我的伪装靠的都是纯纯的手艺跟技术！
t_en: Magic? No. Magic doesn't exist. My disguise is based on pure craftsmanship and skill!

t_ch: …总之，等下次再玩捉迷藏的时候，你肯定抓不到我的！我保证！
t_en: ...Anyway, the next time we play hide-and-seek, you won't be able to find me! I am <unbreak>100%</unbreak> sure of that!

t_ch: 尤利安究竟是怎么伪装的？
t_en: How exactly do you disguise yourself?

t_ch: 呣…这可是秘密哦？可不能轻易告诉{M#大哥哥}{F#大姐姐}……
t_en: Hmm... It's a secret. I'm not going to tell you...

t_ch: 不能告诉我吗？
t_en: Can't you tell me? Please?

t_ch: 当然不能！这可是我们「鼹鼠党」的头号机密之一！
t_en: No! That's one of The Moles' most important secrets!

t_ch: 我作为虎克老大的得力干将，怎么能透露这种重要的信息！{M#大哥哥}{F#大姐姐}还是放弃吧！
t_en: As the right-hand man to Boss Hook, I would never reveal such important information! You might as well give up!

t_ch: 其实是虎克拜托我来问的…
t_en: Actually, Hook sent me...

t_ch: 虎克？…不对，应该是漆黑的虎克大人！真是没大没小！
t_en: Hook? I mean... Pitch-Dark Hook the Great! How dare you address her by just that name!

t_ch: 哼，别想用这种借口来套我的话！虎克老大想知道的话，她早就当面来问我了！
t_en: Hmph, I won't be fooled by that excuse! If Boss Hook wanted to know anything from me, she would ask me herself!

t_ch: 嘿，看你那副沮丧的表情…被我识破了吧！
t_en: Haha, your pathetic face tells me that... I saw through your trick.

t_ch: 哼，真是拗不过你。
t_en: Hmm... You win.

t_ch: 好吧好吧，我放弃啦。
t_en: All right, I give up.

t_ch: 这才对嘛。有的事情，{M#大哥哥}{F#大姐姐}还是不知道为好哦？
t_en: Good. It's better for you to not know everything.

t_ch: 不过，要是你要是能给我带一千包「雪地跳跳糖」过来…我就考虑把「千面」的绝技传授给你！
t_en: If you can bring me a thousand packets of snow-white popping candy... I'll consider teaching you my disguise technique!

t_ch: 没事啦。
t_en: I'm okay.

t_ch: 你先玩吧，不打扰你咯。
t_en: Have fun. See you.

t_ch: 再见！想学「伪装术」的话，记得带跳跳糖过来！
t_en: Bye! If you want to learn the disguise technique, bring me some popping candy!

t_ch: 啊，又见到{M#大哥哥}{F#大姐姐}了！我在执行虎克老大的机密任务呢！
t_en: Ah, it's you again! I'm on a classified mission from Boss Hook!

t_ch: 任务的目标…就是把下层区所有的跳跳糖都搜刮过来，嘿嘿！
t_en: The goal of the mission is to... get my hands on all the popping candy in Underworld. Hehe!

t_ch: 最近过得怎么样？
t_en: How have things been?

t_ch: 咦，{M#大哥哥}{F#大姐姐}问我吗？我的话…最近我的「雪地跳跳糖」又吃完了。
t_en: Oh, do you mean me, {F#miss}{M#mister}? Me, I'm... fresh out of snow-white popping candy.

t_ch: 呃，不是问你这个啦…
t_en: I wasn't asking about that...

t_ch: 嗯…那我最近也没什么别的事情呀。跟着虎克老大执行任务，还有修炼我的伪装技巧！除此以外就没了。
t_en: Oh... well, there's nothing much going on with me. Just questing with Hook, and practicing my camouflage skills! That's about it.

t_ch: 难道最近还有什么大新闻吗？有的话就快告诉我，我要赶紧向虎克老大报告！
t_en: Why, has something really big happened? If so, please tell me — and I'll report back to Hook about it!

t_ch: 「雪地跳跳糖」到底是什么？
t_en: What on earth is this snow-white popping candy?

t_ch: 这你都不知道？这是现在磐岩镇最流行的零食！跳跳糖和别的糖不一样，它会在你的嘴里噼里啪啦地乱跳呢！厉害吧？
t_en: You don't know? It's the most popular snack in all Boulder Town right now! It's not like other candies, because it goes POP POP in your mouth! Awesome, huh?

t_ch: 哼，现在的大人真是一点见识都没有。可惜我现在已经吃完了，等下次我再分你一点吧——不过…你要用一条有用的信息来交换！
t_en: Hmm, grown-ups these days really have no idea. Too bad I've finished it all. You can have some next time — but... you'll have to trade me some information for it!

t_ch: 最近上下层区要解除封锁了。
t_en: The Overworld and the Underworld are going to be reunited.

t_ch: 嗐，这个鼹鼠党早就知道啦！{M#大哥哥}{F#大姐姐}可别把我们当成那种反应迟钝的组织喔！
t_en: Ah, the Moles already knew all that! {F#Miss}{M#Mister}, you should know we're smarter than that!

t_ch: 你要是能再带点跳跳糖过来的话，我就教你怎么搜集下层区最前沿的信息！
t_en: If you can bring me some more popping candy, I'll tell you how to collect the hottest gossip about the Underworld!

t_ch: 我把费斯曼老爹的事摆平了。
t_en: I sorted things out with Fersman.

t_ch: 喔，那可太好了！难怪虎克老大会授予你荣誉队员称号呢。谢谢你，{M#大哥哥}{F#大姐姐}！
t_en: Oh, that's great! No wonder Hook made you an honorary member. Thank you, {F#miss}{M#mister}!

t_ch: 不过，事情都摆平了…虎克老大为什么还哭得那么惨啊？
t_en: But if everything's sorted... Why has Hook been bawling her eyes out?

t_ch: 因为父女情深吧。
t_en: Because a father and daughter's love knows no bounds.

t_ch: …听起来好像发生了很多事。等虎克老大心情好的时候，我去找她打听打听。
t_en: ...Sounds like lots of water under the bridge. I'll wait for Hook to be in a better mood before I ask her about it.

t_ch: 美少女的事你少管。
t_en: Don't stick your beak into ladies' affairs.

t_ch: …你说得对。我还是不瞎打听了。
t_en: ...You're right. I'm not gonna do it anymore.

t_ch: 总之，费斯曼老爹的事就是我们鼹鼠党的事。我身为鼹鼠党二把手，必须好好夸奖你一番，荣誉队员！
t_en: After all, Fersman's problem is the Moles' problem, too. And as the Moles' deputy, I really must praise you, o trusty honorary member!

t_ch: 完了完了……
t_en: It's over... it's all over...

t_ch: 冷静一下，不要着急。
t_en: Calm down.

t_ch: 发生这种事，你要我怎么冷静下来！
t_en: How can I calm down when something like this happened!

t_ch: 完了完了…
t_en: Uh-oh...

t_ch: 你不要跟着起哄了好吗？我是真的很急。
t_en: I'm seriously worried. Can you not make it worse?

t_ch: 发生什么事了？
t_en: What happened?

t_ch: …这你都不知道吗？发生了…大事！
t_en: ...You don't know? Something BIG has happened!

t_ch: …你等等，给我点儿时间。
t_en: ...Hold on, give me some time.

t_ch: 不能忘记的事，不想忘记的事，绝对不可以忘记的事…是什么来着？
t_en: Something I cannot forget... don't want to forget... absolutely must not forget... What was it again?

t_ch: …算了，之后再说吧。你找我有什么事？
t_en: ...No, I'll deal with it some other time. What is it you want to see me about?

t_ch: 我有事想问你…
t_en: There is something I want to ask you...

t_ch: 你到底忘记什么事了？
t_en: What did you forget?

t_ch: 我真的不记得了，只知道是绝对不可以忘记的事…这样，你给我点时间！我从头捋一遍。
t_en: I don't know. What I do know is that it was something I should never forget... Look, give me some time! I'll try to retrace my memory...

t_ch: 就从「我是谁」开始吧。我叫…马克西姆…？
t_en: Let's start with who I am. My name is... Maxime... I think.

t_ch: 不必从这里开始…
t_en: I don't think you need to start from the beginning...

t_ch: 真的有什么大事吗…
t_en: Did something big really happen?

t_ch: 别打断我，我好不容易才把名字想起来……
t_en: Don't interrupt me. I had a hard time just remembering my name...

t_ch: 然后就是，我是做什么的…我应该是个矿工，没错吧？
t_en: And then what do I do...? I'm a miner, right?

t_ch: 我不知道。
t_en: I don't know.

t_ch: 你怎么也不知道？这还怎么往下捋……
t_en: You don't know either? How am I supposed to go on then...

t_ch: 你这身打扮，应该没跑了。
t_en: That's a reasonable conclusion, given how you're dressed.

t_ch: 行，就当我是吧。我叫马克西姆，是个矿工。
t_en: Okay, let's just assume I am. I'm Maxime and I'm a miner.

t_ch: 啊，那接下来呢？
t_en: Ah, what's next?

t_ch: 完了完了……
t_en: It's over... it's all over...

t_ch: 得，又绕回来了。
t_en: Hmm, we are back to square one.

t_ch: 你可真事不关己，都怪你……
t_en: Wow, you look so relaxed. It was your fault, you know...!

t_ch: 你还记得诊所的路吗？
t_en: Do you remember the way to the clinic?

t_ch: 怎么，你觉得我这是病？
t_en: What, do you think I'm ill?

t_ch: 算了，算了…我再想想，肯定能记起来……
t_en: Never mind... I'll go over it all again. I'm sure I can remember something...

t_ch: 算了，你慢慢想吧。
t_en: Well, take your time.

t_ch: 希望下次见面你能想起来…
t_en: I hope you remember something before the next time we meet...

t_ch: …你一说话，我就又忘了。让我一个人静静……
t_en: ...I forgot as soon as you spoke. Now leave me alone...

t_ch: …啊，我想起来了，想起来了！
t_en: Ah, I remember! I remember now!

t_ch: ——是什么来着？话明明都到嘴边了…完了完了……
t_en: Wait! What is it? It was just on the tip of my tongue... Oh, no, no, no...

t_ch: 最近过得怎么样？
t_en: How have things been?

t_ch: 最近？让我想想…好像是有什么重要的事情发生，而且和上下层区有关……
t_en: Lately? Lemme think... Some important stuff happened, I think, and it had to do with the Over and Underworlds...

t_ch: 是说上下层区要重新打通了吗？
t_en: You mean that the Over and Underworlds are opening up?

t_ch: 完了完了…
t_en: Uh-oh...

t_ch: …我正想着呢，别瞎起哄！
t_en: ...I'm thinking! Stop interrupting me!

t_ch: 哎，你一打岔，我又得从头捋一遍了。完了完了……
t_en: Bah, now I have to go through my thoughts again. Oh no...

t_ch: 噢——对的对的对的，就是这件事。这可是件大事！
t_en: Ooh, right! That's right! It was about that. This is major!

t_ch: 呃…不对不对不对。虽然这件事很重要，但我总觉得要说的不是这件事……
t_en: Er... No no no. This matter is major, but I don't think it's what I wanted to say...

t_ch: …嗐，算了吧。想不起来就说明和我没多大关系，不想了。
t_en: ...*sigh* Forget it. If I can't recall it, it just means it has nothing to do with me. I'm done thinking.

t_ch: 唔，到处都找不到那个姐姐…当时好像都没问姐姐叫什么名字呢。
t_en: Mmm... I can't seem to find that lady anywhere... And I don't think I asked her for her name.

t_ch: 话说回来…当时好像都没问姐姐叫什么名字呢。
t_en: ...I didn't even ask her name.

t_ch: 你是埃里克吗？
t_en: Are you Eric?

t_ch: 嗯？没错，我是埃里克…那个，老爹的债务之前应该付清了吧……
t_en: Hmm? Yes, I am Eric... I believe I've already paid off my dad's debt...

t_ch: 你在找谁？
t_en: Who are you looking for?

t_ch: 啊，我在找一位大姐姐，她是一位…那个…银鬃铁卫。您见过这样的人吗？
t_en: Ah, I'm looking for a lady. She is a... Silvermane Guard... Have you seen someone like that?

t_ch: …吓？！你…你不是之前铆钉镇上遇到的{M#大哥哥}{F#大姐姐}吗？
t_en: ...Huh? Didn't we meet in Rivet Town earlier?

t_ch: 真是太好了！我正找你们呢！…咦，铁卫姐姐和你不在一起吗？
t_en: How wonderful! I was looking for you! ...Huh, is that Guard lady with you?

t_ch: 你说布洛妮娅？
t_en: Are you talking about Bronya?

t_ch: 那个姐姐是叫布洛妮娅吗？我记住了！
t_en: Her name was Bronya... Now I remember!

t_ch: 你怎么这么紧张？
t_en: Why are you so nervous?

t_ch: 我…我以为你也是来讨债的……
t_en: I... I thought you were here to collect a debt, too...

t_ch: 但我先前已经替老爹付清债务了，应该没人会再来找麻烦了吧……
t_en: But I've already paid the debt for my papa. There shouldn't be anyone else coming around looking for trouble...

t_ch: 那个铁卫姐姐叫布洛妮娅。
t_en: Her name is Bronya.

t_ch: 布洛妮娅…我记住了！
t_en: Bronya... Got it!

t_ch: 那你可以告诉我她在哪里吗？我想和她说几句话。
t_en: Can you tell me where she is? I'd like to talk with her.

t_ch: 在那之前，我想先和你聊聊…
t_en: Let's have a chat first...

t_ch: 你不会还在当扒手吧？
t_en: Are you still a pickpocket?

t_ch: 怎、怎么会！多亏了铁卫姐姐的奖章，现在我已经帮老爹把债务还清了。
t_en: No-no way! Thanks to the medal the Guard lady gave me, I've been able to help my old man pay off his debt.

t_ch: 我，我答应了那个姐姐，以后绝对不会再偷东西了…必须说到做到……
t_en: I... I promised her that I would never steal again... I must keep my word...

t_ch: 还债？
t_en: Pay off the debt?

t_ch: 你老爹生病了？
t_en: Is your dad sick?

t_ch: 是的，我老爹…他之前生了重病，为了支付医药费，身上背了很多债务。
t_en: Yes, he was seriously ill. He got into a lot of debt due to his medical bills.

t_ch: 如果不能按时还债的话，爸爸就会…所以，所以我才被迫去外面偷东西……
t_en: If papa doesn't pay the debt on time, he would be... So that's why I started to steal...

t_ch: 那你现在在做什么？
t_en: So what do you do now?

t_ch: 唔，我在聚落跟着几个叔叔阿姨学机械修理…
t_en: Well, I'm learning to be a mechanic from some folks in the settlement...

t_ch: 等我学成了，以后就能靠这门手艺赚钱，然后就能帮到老爹他……
t_en: When I master the techniques, I'll be able to make some money with them. Then I can help my dad...

t_ch: 辛苦你了…
t_en: You are doing great...

t_ch: 一切都在变好，加油呀！
t_en: Things will get better. I'm rooting for you!

t_ch: 以前，我给下层区的大家添了许多麻烦…真的很对不起！那些被我偷了东西的人，说不定他们的境况也没有比我好到哪去……
t_en: I'm really sorry about all the trouble I've caused for everyone in the Underworld! Those I stole from could very well be in a worse situation than me...

t_ch: 我现在知道错了！以后我一定要做个有用的人，为大家多做贡献……
t_en: I really regret it! From now on, I will be a good person and be a useful member to society...

t_ch: 你想找布洛妮娅干嘛？
t_en: Why do you want to see Bronya?

t_ch: 因为…因为是那个姐姐给了我宝贵的奖章啊。
t_en: Because... Because she gave me that wonderful medal.

t_ch: 我按布洛妮娅姐姐说的，拿着那个奖章换了钱，这才把老爹的债务都还清了。
t_en: Bronya told me to exchange it for money and that's how I paid off all papa's debts.

t_ch: 而且，在布洛妮娅姐姐之前，根本没有人会那样对待我…大家都嫌弃我手脚不干净，像见了疯地鼠一样躲着我……
t_en: No one had ever treated me so nicely until I met Bronya... Everyone despised me for being a thief and avoided me like I was some kind of rabid gopher...

t_ch: 所以…我想找到布洛妮娅姐姐，当面好好谢谢她。
t_en: So... I want to thank her in person.

t_ch: 如果我见到她，会向她转达的。
t_en: I'll pass on the message when I see her.

t_ch: 真的吗？！…谢谢你，{M#大哥哥}{F#大姐姐}！
t_en: Really!? Thank you!

t_ch: 不知道希儿会怎么想…
t_en: I wonder what Seele will think...

t_ch: 希儿姐姐吗？…唔，虽然希儿姐姐对我很凶，但那是因为我以前犯了不少错误…其实她都是为了我好，这我清楚！
t_en: Seele? Well, she was mean to me because I did a lot of bad things... but I know it was for my own good!

t_ch: 希儿姐姐很有正义感，身手也很好…我长大后也想成为她那样的大人！
t_en: Seele is noble and skilled... I want to be just like her when I grow up!

t_ch: 没事啦。
t_en: I'm okay.

t_ch: 我找到她了就来告诉你。
t_en: I'll let you know when I see her.

t_ch: 拜托你了，{M#大哥哥}{F#大姐姐}！
t_en: Thank you!

t_ch: {M#大哥哥}{F#大姐姐}！你们见到布洛妮娅姐姐了吗？
t_en: Have you seen Bronya?

t_ch: 最近过得怎么样？
t_en: How have things been?

t_ch: 最近的话，我还在找布洛妮娅姐姐…但她似乎一直都没有在附近出现。她到底住在哪里呢？
t_en: I'm still looking for Bronya... But she doesn't seem to be anywhere. Where the heck does she live?

t_ch: 她最近还在地上忙自己的事情。
t_en: She's been busy with work up in the Overworld.

t_ch: 你可以去上层区找她。
t_en: You can go up to the Overworld to look for her.

t_ch: 啊…原来布洛妮娅姐姐住在上层区啊。怪不得一直都找不到她呢……
t_en: Ah... so she lives up in the Overworld, then. No wonder I couldn't find her...

t_ch: 啊…说起来，听大人们说，我们好像很快就能去上面了。如果有机会的话，我一定要坐缆车去找布洛妮娅姐姐。
t_en: Ah... speaking of which, I heard the grown-ups say that we can go up there soon. If I can, I'm definitely gonna try and catch the cable car to find Bronya.

t_ch: {M#大哥哥}{F#大姐姐}，谢谢你告诉我这个消息。我会做好准备的！但首先要照顾好老爹，让他的身体好起来……
t_en: {F#Miss}{M#Mister}, thank you for telling me this news. I'll be ready! But first, I have to look after my dad and make sure he's healthy...

t_ch: 我脸上有什么东西吗？
t_en: Do I have something on my face?

t_ch: 啊…欸？没、没什么！只是，我…我想问你们点事！
t_en: Huh...? No, no. It's just that... there is something I want to ask you!

t_ch: （不理睬他）
t_en: (Ignore him)

t_ch: 那、那个…借、借一步说话！
t_en: Hey... can I talk to you for a second?

t_ch: 你看起来很警惕。
t_en: You look like you have your guard up.

t_ch: …怎么会呢。我、我只是，纯粹对你们有点…好奇。
t_en: ...No, not at all. I'm just... just... a little curious.

t_ch: 什么事？
t_en: What is it?

t_ch: 啊…就、就是……
t_en: Well... um...

t_ch: 啊，还是算、算了，没什么…不好意思啊。
t_en: Never mind, nothing really... Sorry about that.

t_ch: 到底怎么了？
t_en: What's really going on?

t_ch: 你不是扒手吧？
t_en: You're a pickpocket, aren't you?

t_ch: 怎…怎么可能！我要是扒、扒手，奥列格老大哪肯让我进「地火」啊……
t_en: No — no way! I—If I were a pickpocket, Chief Oleg wouldn't let me into Wildfire...

t_ch: 奥列格？
t_en: Oleg?

t_ch: 你、你们不认识老大？
t_en: Y—You don't know the chief?

t_ch: 「地火」？
t_en: Wildfire?

t_ch: 你、你们不知道「地火」？不、不可能，你肯定是在开、开玩笑……
t_en: Y—You don't know what Wildfire is...? You must be kidding...

t_ch: 那你是什么人？
t_en: Who are you?

t_ch: 我…我就是普通的「地火」文员，平时干些…记录工作，还有会、会计活。
t_en: I'm... I'm just a regular bureaucrat for Wildfire. I do... admin and accounting.

t_ch: 我们「地火」，就、就是自发组织起来，维持地下治安的…奥、奥列格是我们组织的老、老大。
t_en: Wildfire i—is a self-organized group for maintaining order in the Underworld... O—Oleg is the leader of the group.

t_ch: 你想和我说什么？
t_en: What do you want to talk to me about?

t_ch: 没…没什么。就是，听说你们是从外、外边来的…是真的吗？
t_en: I heard that you are from the o—outside... Is that true?

t_ch: 你、你们放心！我是「地火」的人，不、不会和别人乱说的。
t_en: Don't worry! I—I won't tell anyone. I'm from Wildfire.

t_ch: 要是、要是不想说，也、也没事。我就是纯粹好、好奇……
t_en: I—If you don't want to tell me, it's okay. I was just c—curious...

t_ch: 是啊，我们来自另一个世界。
t_en: Yes, we're from another world.

t_ch: 啊…？另、另一个世界？
t_en: Huh...? A—Another world?

t_ch: 你、你们别逗我啊。但是你们看起来还挺认、认真……
t_en: You're kidding me... well actually... you seem p—pretty serious...

t_ch: 你想知道这个干什么？
t_en: Why do you want to know about this?

t_ch: 没、没事！就是没见、见过外来者……
t_en: N—Nothing! It's just that I've never met an outsider...

t_ch: 大、大部分地底人一辈子都没见过从外面来的人，觉、觉得奇怪也正常吧……
t_en: M—Most undergrounders have never seen anyone from the outside, not in their entire lives. S—So it's quite normal for me to find you strange...

t_ch: 那、那个，我问完了…没别的事的话，我、我就先告辞……
t_en: Th—That's all I wanted to ask... If it's okay with you, I—I'm gonna go...

t_ch: 没什么事就算了…
t_en: Okay.

t_ch: 好吧，那我走了。
t_en: Okay, see you.

t_ch: 慢、慢走啊。呼……
t_en: T—Take care. Phew...

t_ch: …哎、哎？看我做什么？
t_en: Huh? What are you looking at me for?

t_ch: 我、我没别的意思。各位、各位没事的话就不、不打扰了……
t_en: Sorry, I — I didn't mean to bother you. I'll leave you alone...

t_ch: 最近过得怎么样？
t_en: How have things been?

t_ch: 哦，是、是你们啊。多亏了你、你们，我们终、终于能回到地面上了。
t_en: Oh, i—it's you folks. It's all thanks to y—you that we can f—finally return to the surface.

t_ch: 这就是我、我们「地火」最、最想看到的。但、但是，各种麻烦事又、又层出不穷……
t_en: This is what the W—Wildfire wanted m—most. B—But, plenty of troubles h—have risen...

t_ch: 什么麻烦事？
t_en: What troubles?

t_ch: 不会又要我跑腿了吧，唉…
t_en: Do I have to run errands again...?

t_ch: 哪、哪儿能呢！你、你们已经帮了我们太多。这是我们内、内部的事，我们自、自己会解决。
t_en: O—Of course not! Y—You have already done so much for us. This is an in—internal affair. We will d—deal with it ourselves.

t_ch: 毕、毕竟，我们「地火」接管下、下层区也很多年了……
t_en: A—After all, we have been in charge of the Underworld for years...

t_ch: 我们中的一些成、成员，本来就对大守护者有意见，现在更是不、不想乖乖听她的话了。
t_en: Some of our m—members have always had a problem with the Supreme Guardian. Now, they've found a better excuse to n—not to listen to her.

t_ch: 不过，没关系…我会配合奥、奥列格老大，想办法说、说服他们的。
t_en: Still, everything is alright... Together with O—Oleg, I'll try to c—convince them.

t_ch: 嗯…现在应该只缺「逻辑语言中枢」了吧。
t_en: Hmm... The only thing missing is a language module, right?

t_ch: 这该上哪儿弄啊……
t_en: Where do we get that?

t_ch: 「语言逻辑中枢」？
t_en: A language module?

t_ch: 是啊，就缺这个了，但这种模组现在可不好弄啊…哎，真让人头大。
t_en: Yeah, it's all that's missing. It's hard to get hold of one... *sigh* What a headache.

t_ch: （不打扰她）
t_en: (Leave her alone)

t_ch: …好烦好烦。总不能从史瓦罗大佬身上拆下来用吧，真是的。
t_en: ... This is so annoying. I can't just take Svarog's, can I? *sigh*

t_ch: ……哎、哎？！你什么时候凑过来的？没听到我刚才说的话吧……
t_en: ...Hmm? When did you get here? Did you hear what I just said...?

t_ch: 应该没有吧。
t_en: I don't think so.

t_ch: …那最好是。
t_en: You better not have.

t_ch: 你怎么反应那么大？
t_en: Why are you acting like it's a big deal?

t_ch: …看你这反应，明明就听到了吧！
t_en: ... So you did hear it!

t_ch: 唉，算了算了，让你知道也无所谓，反正也不是什么了不得的秘密。
t_en: *sigh* Whatever. I guess it's okay if you know. It isn't really a huge secret anyway.

t_ch: 是张生面孔呢…找我有什么事吗？
t_en: You seem new here... What can I do for you?

t_ch: 我想问你…
t_en: I'm wondering...

t_ch: 你是机械师？
t_en: Are you a mechanic?

t_ch: 不然呢？除了机械师，也没人会制作机器人了吧？
t_en: Of course. No one but a mechanic could make robots, could they?

t_ch: …不过硬要说的话，那群流浪者里也有不少人有这种手艺啦。
t_en: ...Well, actually, many people among the vagrants also possess that skill.

t_ch: 但我和他们可不一样！那些家伙制造机器人就是为了办什么「改造机器人争霸赛」，让自己造出来的东西互殴到底…我反正无法理解那种趣味。
t_en: But I'm different from them! They make robots for the Modified Robot Fight — the robots they make fight each other... I don't get the fun in it.

t_ch: 你要「逻辑语言中枢」做什么？
t_en: What do you need a language module for?

t_ch: 这…你对别人的兴趣就这么好奇嘛？
t_en: It's... Why are you being so nosey?

t_ch: 告诉你吧…其实我正在试着制造一台能与人类进行「情感互动」的机器人。
t_en: Okay, I'll tell you... I'm trying to build a robot that can interact with humans emotionally.

t_ch: ——不是那种只会按照预先定好的程序、你一问它一答的机器！是真正能理解人的语言，并且能像正常人类一样说话、做事的机器人。
t_en: Not the kind of machine that only follows a program or answers the question you ask — A robot that understands human language, and speaks and acts like a normal human being.

t_ch: 为什么要制作这种机器人？
t_en: Why do you want to build something like that?

t_ch: 为啥？当然是…因为梦想呀！
t_en: Why? Because it's my dream, of course!

t_ch: 造出带有人类情感的机器人…那就是我作为业余机械师的终极梦想！
t_en: I want to create a robot with emotions... it's my ultimate goal as an amateur mechanic!

t_ch: 你是要制作仿生人吗？
t_en: Are you making an android?

t_ch: 仿生人？那是什么，听着让人怪不舒服的…我现在只想给机器人搭载逻辑语言模块，然后再在这个基础上做些优化改进。
t_en: Android? What's that? It sounds weird... Right now, all I want to do is to equip the robot with a language module and do some additional optimization and improvement.

t_ch: 在我看来，机械结构可比人体美妙多了…我才不会傻到把机器人做成和人类一样的外观呢，真无趣。
t_en: In my opinion, mechanical structures are much more beautiful than the human body... I'm not stupid enough to try to make a robot look like a human. How boring would that be?

t_ch: 我还以为是能驾驶的人形机甲呢。
t_en: I thought you were making a humanoid mech that could be driven.

t_ch: 那种事我也考虑过…听说男孩子都梦想着能驾驶人形机甲作战？
t_en: I've thought about that... I heard that boys all dream of fighting in mechs.

t_ch: 我也能理解他们的浪漫幻想啦。不过对我自己来说，能让机器人拥有近似人类的感情，才是最值得投入精力的研究目标……
t_en: I get why they think that's cool. But for me, the most worthwhile research is to make robots that have human emotions...

t_ch: 总之就是这样啦。你还有别的问题吗？
t_en: Anyway, that's all. Do you have any other questions?

t_ch: 没什么了。
t_en: Um... Nothing.

t_ch: 没什么，你继续研究机械吧。
t_en: Nevermind. You can go back to your mechanical research.

t_ch: 哦，你又来啦？我还没找到「语言逻辑中枢」呢……
t_en: Hey, it's you again? I haven't found a language module...

t_ch: 怎么，今天有什么事吗？
t_en: What's up today?

t_ch: 最近过得怎么样？
t_en: How have things been?

t_ch: 唔，怎么突然这么问？最近难道又有什么大新闻吗？
t_en: Why are you asking that all of a sudden? Is something big going on at the moment?

t_ch: 你不知道吗？大矿区出乱子了！
t_en: You haven't heard? There's chaos at the Great Mine!

t_ch: 天啊，真的吗？我一直在专心研究自己的机器人，完全不知道这件事！
t_en: Oh no, really? I've been so focused on my robot that I haven't heard a thing!

t_ch: 我记得大矿区有很多机器人在协助矿民工作…怎么样，那些小机器人没受伤吧？
t_en: I remember there being lots of support robots in the Great Mine... What about them, are those little robots all okay?

t_ch: 啊，其实是上下层解除封锁了…
t_en: Ah, actually the Overworld and the Underworld are being reunited...

t_ch: 你不知道吗？上下层解除封锁了！
t_en: Haven't you heard? The Overworld and the Underworld are being reunited!

t_ch: 你不知道吗？今天洞螈肉半价！
t_en: Haven't you heard? Olm meat is half price today!

t_ch: 天啊，真的吗？我一直在专心研究自己的机器人，完全不知道这件事！
t_en: Oh no, really? I've been so focused on my robot that I haven't heard a thing!

t_ch: 不过我也不爱吃洞螈肉…这「新闻」倒是和我关系不大啦。还有什么其他新闻吗？
t_en: Olm meat isn't my favorite anyway... So this "news" has nothing to do with me. Got any other news for me?

t_ch: 啊～原来如此。这件事我倒是听别人聊过，不过我还以为他们是开玩笑呢。
t_en: Oh, so that's how it is~ I heard some people chatting about this, but I thought they were only joking.

t_ch: 上下层区解除封锁…在我看来是件好事啊。下层区能搞到的机械零件就那么点，要不就是垃圾堆里翻出来的残次品……
t_en: Reuniting the Overworld and the Underworld... seems like a good idea to me. There's hardly any spare parts down in the Underworld. Just piles of defective junk...

t_ch: 上层区就不一样了。听说那里有一家远近闻名的「机械屋」——我老早就想去那里看看了！搞不好在那里能弄到我想要的「逻辑语言中枢」，还有其它珍稀的零件……
t_en: It's different in the Overworld. I heard there's a famous "workshop" up there — I've always wanted to see it! Maybe I can get the language module I've been looking for up there — or other rare parts...

t_ch: 事不宜迟，我这就开始收拾行李！谢谢你提供的宝贵情报啦！
t_en: Without further ado, I'll start packing! Thanks so much for this valuable intel!

t_ch: 哦，你来啦！你瞧，我的机器人已经造好啦。
t_en: Oh, here you are! Look! I've finished building my robot.

t_ch: 您好，欢迎光临歌德宾馆！
t_en: Hello! Welcome to the Goethe Hotel!

t_ch: 请问您是要住店，还是想来杯宾馆特色下午茶？
t_en: Are you staying with us? Or would you like to try our special afternoon tea?

t_ch: 我是慕名而来观光的。
t_en: I'm here as I was attracted by the hotel's fame.

t_ch: 宾馆只是开得比较久，在贝洛伯格留了点虚名而已。
t_en: The hotel has just been here for a long time and it became a bit famous in Belobog.

t_ch: 来者都是客，你也不用太拘谨，尽管逛吧，只要别打扰住店的客人就行。
t_en: Make yourself at home. Feel free to take a tour, as long as you are not interrupting the other guests.

t_ch: 我路过的，随便看看
t_en: I am only passing by. I'll have a look around.

t_ch: 正好我眼下也没什么事要做，你要不介意，可以跟我寒暄几句。
t_en: I happen to have some free time. If you don't mind, you can chat with me.

t_ch: 我想订个客房休息会儿。
t_en: I would like to reserve a room.

t_ch: 行，麻烦来前台登记下你的身份信息。
t_en: Sure, please register at the reception.

t_ch: 我想喝下午茶。
t_en: I would like to try your afternoon tea.

t_ch: 噢，就在大堂两边，先找个沙发随便坐，我这就去安排。
t_en: Oh, it's on the sides of the lobby. Find yourself a sofa and take a seat. I'll arrange it.

t_ch: 我打小就在这里长大，不敢说对行政区了如指掌，但这宾馆，还有宾馆附近有过什么变化，又有过什么样的故事，我多少也知道点。
t_en: This is where I grew up. I wouldn't say that I know all about the Administrative District. But I do know the history and goings-on around these parts fairly well.

t_ch: 你要是感兴趣，尽管问我就是了。
t_en: Just let me know if you are interested.

t_ch: 我想问……
t_en: I want to ask...

t_ch: 尽管问吧！有问必答也是待客之道。
t_en: Sure! Answering the guests' questions is a way to show your hospitality.

t_ch: 老歌德和「歌德宾馆」…
t_en: About Old Goethe and Goethe Hotel...

t_ch: 噢，你对这个感兴趣？歌德是我的姓氏，宾馆是我家的产业，说起来都是些陈芝麻烂谷子的老事了。
t_en: Oh, that's what you're interested in? Goethe is my family name and Goethe Hotel is my family business. Well, it all began a long, long time ago.

t_ch: 歌德家族以前也算兴盛一时，祖辈们投资了不少产业，可惜因为经济不景气，终归还是没落了。先辈心灰意冷，卖空所有家产以后搬到了下层区居住。
t_en: The Goethe family used to be prosperous. My ancestors invested in many industries. Unfortunately, due to poor economics, our family declined. Frustrated, my ancestors sold all of their properties and moved to the Underworld.

t_ch: 但好在家族后来又出了一位志向高远的伟人——那位先辈从下层白手起家，振兴祖业，又成功把歌德宾馆开回了上层。
t_en: Luckily later on another ancestor from my family started empty-handed and revived our family business. Once again, Goethe Hotel was moved back to the Overworld.

t_ch: 接待过什么特别的住客吗？
t_en: Have you received any special guests?

t_ch: 那要看你是怎么定义「特别的住客」的了。住过这儿的客人一茬接一茬的，的确留下过不少故事。
t_en: Well, it depends on how you define special guest. Many guests have stayed with us. Indeed there are a lot of stories.

t_ch: 比如说——有位逃婚的大小姐穿着睡衣从二楼客房跳窗逃跑，听说她自此下落不明……
t_en: For example, a lady from a rich family escaped her wedding and jumped out of the window on the second floor in her pajamas. No one ever heard from her since...

t_ch: 有对剧院明星夫妻，看起来很恩爱，但来宾馆度蜜月的时候，两个人却分别开了两个房间……
t_en: There was a couple who were theater celebrities. They appeared as though they were in love but they stayed in different rooms when they were here for their honeymoon...

t_ch: 还有位女士，经常在她家人的工作时间过来开个单人间。她总是待在房间里画画，画完就会大哭一场，也不知道是经历了什么……
t_en: And there was a lady who would always stay in a single room when her family was at work. She was always drawing in her room. And she would cry every time she finished drawing. I wonder what she's been through...

t_ch: 我们这行就是这样，时间长了总能碰上些稀罕事儿。
t_en: It happens a lot in our industry. We always encounter weird things, especially when we've been in the industry for a long enough time.

t_ch: 附近有什么好吃的吗？
t_en: Any recommendations on food around here?

t_ch: 哈哈，不是我自夸，我们宾馆的下午茶是贝洛伯格的一绝。
t_en: Haha, I'm not trying to brag, but our afternoon tea is a specialty of Belobog.

t_ch: 有几道甜品，整个行政区里就我们家能做得出来，在外面你压根吃不到。每到节假日，就有不少年轻人特意过来，人多的时候还要排队呢。
t_en: There are a few desserts that only we can make. You can't eat them anywhere else in the Administrative District. Many young people visit us during holidays. Sometimes people have to line up.

t_ch: 这附近有什么景点推荐吗？
t_en: Any recommendation on sightseeing around here?

t_ch: 我推荐你去西区逛逛，西区以文艺气息而闻名，黄金歌剧院就在那儿，剧院明星、限定周边，应有尽有。
t_en: I'd recommend the Western District, which is known for its artistic atmosphere. The Golden Theater is right there. You'll find everything from celebrities to merchandise.

t_ch: 不过你得注意，别赶在首演日去，像《白衣之后》和《星尘玫瑰》这种粉丝多的，会把你挤到怀疑人生……
t_en: But be careful, don't go on their premiere day. For example, shows with many fans like Beneath the White Cloak and Stardust Rose will make the area totally over-crowded...

t_ch: 最好是自己开车去，你要是没车…从宾馆一出门就是阿丽萨大道，搭电车或者沿着轨道步行都很方便。
t_en: It's best to drive there. If you don't have a car... Alisa Avenue is right outside the hotel. You can take a tram or walk there along the track.

t_ch: 我没什么想问的了。
t_en: I don't have any other questions.

t_ch: 谢谢，还是让我自己转转吧。
t_en: Thank you. I'll walk around by myself.

t_ch: 欢迎，有空可以常来店里坐坐。
t_en: Welcome. Please visit us sometime.

t_ch: 您好，欢迎光临歌德宾馆！
t_en: Hello! Welcome to the Goethe Hotel!

t_ch: 是你啊，这次来可不是来观光的吧？是来住店、吃饭，还是特意来找老头子我聊聊的？
t_en: It's you. You're not here for sightseeing, are you? Are you here to stay or for our afternoon tea? Or did you come here to chat with me?

t_ch: 想找你闲聊几句。
t_en: I'd like to chat with you.

t_ch: 行啊，我平时也没什么事，难得有人愿意来陪我说会儿话。
t_en: Sure. I'm usually quite free. I'd love to have a chat with someone.

t_ch: 你想聊点什么？
t_en: What do you want to chat about?

t_ch: 最近过得怎么样？
t_en: How have things been?

t_ch: 你是问宾馆的生意呢，还是问老头子我啊？宾馆的话还好，不算特别忙。至于我，这日子倒是挺有盼头。
t_en: Do you mean how's business been, or how I've been doing? The hotel's fine, not too busy. As for me, I'm looking forward to good times.

t_ch: 当然是关心你啦！
t_en: Of course I mean you!

t_ch: 噢，谢谢，我最近还不错，甚至称得上是「意气风发」了，哈哈。也不怕你笑话，这两天我还在想怎么把白发遮一遮。
t_en: Oh thanks, I've been pretty good recently — "full of beans" even, you could say, haha! And I'm not afraid of your jokes. I've been thinking about how I can cover up this white hair of mine.

t_ch: 过不了多久就能见到下层区的亲戚朋友们了…十几年过去了，大家肯定都变化很大吧？
t_en: Soon we'll be able to see friends and relatives from down in the Underworld again... Things must be quite different after a dozen or more years, right?

t_ch: 是有什么好事将近吗？
t_en: Are good times on the horizon?

t_ch: 好事将近？你猜得倒准。现在这城里发生了不少事…我听我认识的一些朋友说，你好像也出了不少力？
t_en: Good times? Correct. There's lots going on in the city right now... I hear from friends of mine that you seem to be up to lots, too?

t_ch: 总之啊，过不了多久我就能和下层区的亲戚朋友们见面了。这么多年过去了，也不知道他们过得好不好……
t_en: Anyway, soon I'll be able to see friends and relatives from down in the Underworld. After all these years, I hope they've been doing well...

t_ch: 到了我这个年纪，很多亲戚朋友都在慢慢走出我的生活。有时候我也不免怀念从前的青葱岁月……
t_en: At my age, I have lots of friends and families leaving my life. Sometimes I can't help but miss those halcyon days gone by...

t_ch: 哈哈，在你这种年轻人面前，还是不谈这个了。好好生活吧。
t_en: Haha, I shouldn't be talking about such stuff with a young'un like you. Live well!

t_ch: 想聊聊「正当权益会社」…
t_en: About the Rightful Rights Society...

t_ch: 当然可以。你想了解什么？
t_en: Sure. What do you want to know?

t_ch: 「会社」的职责是？
t_en: What's the Society's mission?

t_ch: 「正当权益会社」——顾名思义，我们的目标便是维护每个人的「正当权益」。
t_en: The Rightful Rights Society — as the name implies, our goal is to uphold the rightful rights of every individual.

t_ch: 简单来说，就是让每个人都能暂时逃离家庭和责任的束缚，自由自在地发展自己的兴趣爱好、或者只是单纯地休息一下……
t_en: In short, we can get everyone to temporarily escape from the bindings of family and duty, to freely develop their hobbies, or just to take a break...

t_ch: …总之，毫无疑问，我们是个造福社会的公益组织。听了我方才的描述，想必你已经完全理解「会社」的职责了吧。
t_en: ...Anyways, we are undoubtedly an organization established for the good of the Society. After listening to my explanation, you should have a full understanding of the Society's mission now.

t_ch: 原来你还有这种隐藏身份…
t_en: So this is your secret identity...

t_ch: 没错。毕竟我们平时都需要隐秘行动，所以不能在外声张自己的身份——这也是我最初请你帮忙时有所犹豫的原因。
t_en: That's right. We usually act in secrecy and don't announce our identities, which is why I hesitated to ask for your help.

t_ch: 我可以享受何种权利？
t_en: What kind of rights?

t_ch: 首先，如果你感到自己需要一些休闲时间却脱不开身，你就可以求助社友，拜托他们替你打掩护；其次，你也可以在有空时协助配合其他成员。
t_en: Firstly, if you ever feel you need some time to relax but can't get away from your duties, you can ask other Society members to help cover for you. Secondly, you can also cover for other members whenever you can.

t_ch: 当然，这并不意味着你可以随便差使社友…要想享受权利，你就必须有过帮助他人的记录才行。
t_en: This certainly doesn't mean you get to order other members about... To enjoy the rights, you must first have a record of helping others.

t_ch: 一次帮助可抵两次求助——我们鼓励大家相互支援，一起享受休闲时光。
t_en: Helping others once grants you two requests for help. We encourage everyone to support each other so that everyone can enjoy some leisure time.

t_ch: 不过，你似乎从来没有在联络群中提请过他人帮助…但你放心，对于这种情况，我们也有备案。
t_en: It seems like you've never asked for help in the group chat, though... Don't worry. We have measures in place for such cases.

t_ch: 每隔一段时间，我们就会盘点一次会员们未用尽的请求支援余额，并发送短信提醒会员折现。
t_en: We periodically review members who have not used their request quotas, and send them text reminders to exchange those for cash.

t_ch: 还能折现？
t_en: I can exchange that for cash?

t_ch: 呵呵，当然…毕竟，请求他人帮助是你的权利。如果你因各种缘故未能行使权利，我们自然会想办法弥补你的。
t_en: You bet... After all, it's your right to request help from others. If you are unable to exercise your right due to various reasons, we have to find a way to compensate you.

t_ch: 总之，只要记住我们会定期给你发送短信便是。如果收到了，就不妨来找老头子我问问看吧。
t_en: Anyway, just bear in mind that you will receive messages from us regularly. If you receive one, please don't hesitate to ask me about it.

t_ch: 我明白了。
t_en: I understand.

t_ch: 呵呵…愿你在「会社」得以享受闲暇时光。
t_en: Hehe... I hope you get to enjoy some leisure time in the Society.

t_ch: 哟，你们回来啦。怎么样，累不累？奥列格大哥没为难你们吧？
t_en: Ah, you're back. How did it go? Are you tired? How did Oleg treat you?

t_ch: 没事没事，他人挺好的。
t_en: He was nice to us.

t_ch: 哈哈，这你可说对了！奥列格大哥虽然平时总板着脸，但其实可照顾大家了。
t_en: Haha, that's true! Although Oleg usually looks quite serious, he takes good care of everyone.

t_ch: 一天到晚跑腿，确实受不了…
t_en: My legs are so sore after running around for the whole day...

t_ch: 哈哈…真是辛苦你了，不过能者多劳嘛！
t_en: Haha... With great power comes great responsibility!

t_ch: 好啦，你跑了一天，肯定累坏了吧，快回房间歇歇！我这就去下厨，等下做好就给你把饭端上去！
t_en: Well, you must be exhausted after running around the whole day. Take a rest in your room. I'll bring the food over when it's ready!

t_ch: 你真是太热情了。
t_en: You are very nice.

t_ch: 辛苦你啦。
t_en: Thank you.

t_ch: 哈哈哈，不麻烦！既然来到我们「歌德大饭店」，那大家就都是朋友了！
t_en: Hahaha, you're welcome! We are all friends now that you're staying at the Goethe Grand Hotel!

t_ch: 哎呀！你瞧我这记性，都忘了问了…被褥枕头合不合适？房间的温度呢？地髓炉没出故障吧？
t_en: Oh! I almost forgot... How do you find the bed and pillow? And what about the room temperature? Is the Geomarrow Furnace working fine?

t_ch: 要是还有什么其他需要，尽管和我说啊！
t_en: If you need anything, please let me know!

t_ch: 我想和你聊聊…
t_en: Can we talk...?

t_ch: 好呀，现在正好不是很忙。
t_en: Sure. I'm not too busy right now.

t_ch: 有什么菜式推荐吗？
t_en: What dishes would you recommend?

t_ch: 推荐菜？来，你把柜台边上那本食评册拿来，看看对里边哪个有兴趣，我给你好好讲讲——这可都是我的得意之作！
t_en: Dishes? Check the food review book on the wardrobe and see what interests you. They are all my masterpieces!

t_ch: …呃，基本上都是。
t_en: ...Well, basically everything.

t_ch: 「杂菜汤」？
t_en: Wild Herb Soup?

t_ch: 对，这道菜的主要食材是洞穴里的苔藓，清爽酸甜，特别推荐用来下饭。虽然卖相不好，但据大家反映口感确实没问题。
t_en: Yes, moss in the cave is the main ingredient. It is refreshing, sour and sweet, and especially good to open your appetite. Although it doesn't look too appealing, the taste is just fine according to our customers' feedback.

t_ch: 不过，这苔藓实在是不太好挖，没办法作为常驻供应就是了……
t_en: However, the moss is really not easy to dig. This dish is not always available...

t_ch: 「铁罐焖螈肉」？
t_en: Iron Pot Braised Olm?

t_ch: 啊，不要看这个…看下面这个——改良型！这道菜是专门用了铁罐来闷炖洞螈肉，这样的话肉的口感会非常好…这是我从书上学来的，哈哈哈。
t_en: Ah, not this one... look at the one below — this is the modified version! We specifically use an iron pot to braise olm meat. That way the meat becomes really tender... I learned it from a book, hahaha.

t_ch: 大家都反映这道菜非常好吃，所以很快就上架供应啦。你要是馋了的话，我现在就可以做！
t_en: People love this dish. It should be available very soon. I can cook some for you if you are hungry.

t_ch: 「蜜饯菇托辣椒」？
t_en: Candied mushrooms with pepper?

t_ch: 哦，这是道冷菜！餐前开胃用的。
t_en: Oh, it's a cold dish, as an appetizer.

t_ch: 你能吃辣吗？这里边用了铁锤辣椒碎提味，不习惯辣口的话可不要轻易尝试啊。
t_en: Can you eat spicy food? I use hammer pepper powder to enhance the flavor. Don't try this if you can't take spicy.

t_ch: 「鼠肉冻」？
t_en: Rat Jelly?

t_ch: 啊…这道菜已经改名成「风味肉冻」啦！这可是磐岩镇的特色菜哦，我强烈建议你尝试一下。
t_en: Well... I've renamed this Flavorful Jelly! This is a specialty of Boulder Town. Highly recommend!

t_ch: 只要你别被原来的名字吓跑就行…哈哈哈。
t_en: Don't get scared away by its original name... Hahaha.

t_ch: 我想看看其他的…
t_en: Any other dishes...

t_ch: 至于其他的，就先不用看啦…那些菜做出来效果都不是很好，我还在想办法改进呢。实在不好意思呀。
t_en: That's all for now... The rest of the dishes don't come out very nice. I'm still working on them. I'm so sorry.

t_ch: 平时客人多吗？
t_en: Do you usually have a lot of guests?

t_ch: 客人是不少，但是基本都是来吃饭的，住店客人相对少一些……
t_en: Many people come here for our food. But not many people stay in our hotel...

t_ch: 毕竟大部分都是磐岩镇本地的熟面孔嘛。大家收工之后经常过来打个尖儿、歇歇脚，倒也挺热闹的。
t_en: After all, most of our guests are from Boulder Town. People would gather here after work to chill and chat with each other. It's pretty lively.

t_ch: 我不光是饭店老板，还是这儿的厨师长哦！很多客人都是冲着我的手艺来的。
t_en: I'm the owner and also the chef of the hotel! Many people come here because of my cooking.

t_ch: 你们外来者见多识广，要是饭菜上有什么喜好，可以多跟我讲讲。我也能趁机跟你们多学两手！
t_en: You outsiders are certainly very well-traveled. Let me know if you have any preferences for food, maybe I can also learn something from you!

t_ch: 这饭店也归「地火」管吗？
t_en: Is this hotel under the administration of Wildfire?

t_ch: 哈哈哈！瞧你们这话说的，把「地火」当作什么啦。
t_en: Hahaha! What do you think Wildfire is?

t_ch: 「地火」只是在银鬃铁卫撤走之后，下层区人自发筹建的维和组织而已。一定要和我们饭店扯上关系的话…我想大概是「客人」吧。
t_en: Wildfire is just a peacekeeping organization started by people in the Underworld after the withdrawal of the Silvermane Guards. If there is a relationship between us... I would probably say they are our guests.

t_ch: 加入「地火」的可都是热心肠的好人哪，奥列格可不会允许游手好闲的小混混加入组织。我有个侄子也在里边工作呢，听说每天还挺忙的。
t_en: The people who join Wildfire are all kindhearted, good folk. Oleg wouldn't let any lazy good-for-nothings join. I have a nephew who works for Wildfire, he says they are really busy every day.

t_ch: 能说说上层的「歌德宾馆」吗？
t_en: Can you tell me about the Goethe Hotel in Overworld?

t_ch: 当然可以！不过我得先想想，从哪里开始说比较好呢……
t_en: Sure! Let me think where I should begin with...

t_ch: 先简单说说我们歌德家族吧！毕竟这两家饭店都是我们家族产业。据说我们家族从寒潮之前就存在了，还和大守护者阿丽萨•兰德关系不错哩。
t_en: I'll start with the Goethe family! After all, both hotels are our family business. It is said that our family was already here before the Eternal Freeze. We had a good relationship with Alisa Rand, the Supreme Guardian.

t_ch: 所以，我们家族当时也算是个首屈一指的大家族——现在的「朗道」家族，你听过吗？我猜啊，那阵子我们估计和他家现在差不多！
t_en: Our family used to be very influential. Have you heard of the Landau family? I guess we were similar to what they are today!

t_ch: 哇，名门望族啊！
t_en: Wow, a prestigious family!

t_ch: 哈哈哈，那都是过去的事了，不值一提。
t_en: Hahaha, that's all in the past. It's not worth mentioning.

t_ch: 你别说，我还认识朗道家的人呢。
t_en: I do know the Landau family.

t_ch: 真的？那你现在又认识我们歌德家的人了，恭喜你！哈哈哈。
t_en: Really? Now you know the Goethe family too. Congratulations! Hahaha.

t_ch: 到后来，家族里出了几个败家子，把家产都败光咯。不过好在后边又有人努力重振家业，这才在这下层区开了家大饭店。
t_en: After that, several scoundrels in the family lost all of our wealth. Fortunately, later on, someone revived our family business and they opened a hotel in the Underworld.

t_ch: 居然有这么悠久的历史…
t_en: I didn't know it had such a long history...

t_ch: 确实啊，我估计得有几百年了吧。爹娘也没和我具体讲过，只说这饭店是祖上传下来的遗产。
t_en: True. Hundreds of years. My parents never told me the details. They just said this hotel was a legacy from my ancestors.

t_ch: 那为什么在地上还有一家？
t_en: Then why is there another one in the Overworld?

t_ch: 哎呀，你别急！这不就快要讲到了嘛。
t_en: Be patient! I was about to talk about that.

t_ch: 后来，我们这饭店生意越做越大，就有人想要去上层区拓展业务咯。
t_en: As our hotel's business grew, some people wanted to expand the business to the Overworld.

t_ch: 没想到啊，这位歌德可是个大能人，居然在行政区最豪华的地段拿下了商铺！所以，上层区的「歌德宾馆」就是这么来的。
t_en: That Goethe must be very talented to be able to buy a shop in the busiest area of the Administrative District! That's how the Goethe Hotel in the Overworld was started.

t_ch: 不过，我们家族也因此分了家，分别成为上下层区两支啦。
t_en: However, our family was divided ever since then, and now there are two separate branches in the Overworld and Underworld.

t_ch: 那你认识上层区的老歌德吗？
t_en: Do you know Old Goethe from the Overworld?

t_ch: 当然认识啊！虽然我刚说分了家，但我们两支还是经常来往的。像什么煦日节啊之类的节日，我们都会举办家族聚会呢。
t_en: Of course! Although our family was divided, we do keep in touch. We hold family gatherings during festivals such as the Solwarm Festival.

t_ch: 至于你说的那个老歌德…按辈分论的话，我和他其实是同辈呢，哈哈哈！
t_en: As for the Old Goethe you mentioned... According to seniority, we are actually the same generation, hahaha!

t_ch: 不过自从上下层区封锁之后，我们就再也没聚过了。这都过去多少年了呢……
t_en: But we haven't been able to get together since the Overworld and the Underworld were blocked. It's been many years...

t_ch: 没事了。
t_en: That's all.

t_ch: 谢谢你，你先忙吧。
t_en: Thank you. I shouldn't keep you.

t_ch: 甭客气，来了下层区就都是我的客人嘛。有什么需要尽管说！
t_en: No worries. You are now my guest since you are in the Underworld. If you need anything, just let me know!

t_ch: 哟，今天忙完啦？我这儿正好做了锅蘑菇汤…你先歇会，晚点记得下来尝尝口味如何！
t_en: Yo, have you finished your work today? I just made a pot of mushroom soup... Take a break first and come try it later!

t_ch: 最近过得怎么样？
t_en: How have things been?

t_ch: 瞧你问的，真是时候！这不，我前阵子才收到上层区亲戚的信呢。这都多少年没收到过信啦，时间过得可真快啊……
t_en: You've got great timing! Why, I just got a letter from a relative up in the Overworld. It's been years since I heard from them. Time really flies...

t_ch: 之前收不到信吗？
t_en: Could you not get letters before?

t_ch: 是啊，自打大守护者封锁上下层区之后，别说人想往返地面，就连信都不让寄咯。
t_en: Yeah, ever since the Supreme Guardian blockaded the Underworlds, nobody was allowed to go back and forth between them — not even the mail.

t_ch: 这都快十年了，我终于收到第一封信…看来贝洛伯格是要变天了！你看看，我这亲戚信里也说了：「大守护者马上就要解除封锁政策」……
t_en: It's been almost ten years, and I've finally received my first letter... Looks like Belobog is back, bay-bee! Check it out, my relative even said in their letter, "The Supreme Guardian is lifting the blockade soon"...

t_ch: 信里说了什么？
t_en: What does the letter say?

t_ch: 哎呀，这信里说的东西可真不少。不过除了些家长里短，这还有个大新闻呢！你看看，我这亲戚信里说了：「大守护者马上就要解除封锁政策」……
t_en: Ah, there's lots to go over in this letter. But besides all the family gossip, there's some big news! Check it out, they said here, "The Supreme Guardian is lifting the blockade soon"...

t_ch: 真要是这么回事啊，到时候我就歇业几天，收拾行李到上层区探亲去！哈哈哈。
t_en: If that's the case, I'm gonna take a few days off, pack up my things, and go up to the Overworld to see some family! Hahaha.

t_ch: 哎，这都多少年了，终于能见上一面…真不容易哪。
t_en: Ah, after all these years, I can finally go up... all these years...

t_ch: 你后来买到礼物了吗？
t_en: Did you manage to buy a gift?

t_ch: 噢，你是说拍卖会那事吧！买到啦买到啦，多谢你关心。
t_en: Oh, you mean at the auction! Yup, I got something. Thanks for remembering.

t_ch: 当时我不是没拍下那只大熊玩偶嘛，后来我又想到个法子——娜塔莎大夫心灵手巧，又喜欢照顾小孩，搞不好能让她帮忙出出主意哩。
t_en: I didn't bid on the big old bear at the time, but thought of something later — Natasha is ingenious, and loves taking care of kids. She'd have a back-up plan for me, I was sure.

t_ch: 结果啊，我这一问就把事情解决了！娜塔莎三下五除二就给我缝了只兔子玩偶，可爱得很嘞。我把这玩偶送给侄女，她也可喜欢了。
t_en: And sure enough, she did! Natasha sewed up a rabbit doll for me just like that, and it was so cute. I gave it to my niece, and she loved it.

t_ch: 哎，娜塔莎姑娘可真是个好医生啊。我们磐岩镇能有这样一位大夫，那真是我们的福气！
t_en: Ahhh, Natasha is such a good doctor. We're blessed to have someone like her here in Boulder Town!

t_ch: 说到这个，奥列格大哥也是我们饭店的老客户了，他尤其喜欢吃「铁罐焖螈肉」——这也是我们的招牌菜，建议你有机会尝试一下！
t_en: Speaking of which, Oleg is also our regular customer. "Iron Pot Braised Olm," our signature dish, is his favorite. You should try it!

t_ch: 「矿灯」已经停止鉴定服务了，详情都在公告上面，请您挪步……
t_en: "Miner's Lamp" has stopped all appraisal services. The announcement has all the details. Please leave...

t_ch: 鉴定？鉴定什么？
t_en: Appraisal? Of what?

t_ch: 公告？什么公告？
t_en: Announcement? What announcement?

t_ch: …你们是来故意捣乱的吗？
t_en: ...Are you here to cause trouble?

t_ch: 嗯？等会儿…你们是从「外面」来的吧？你们的事，大街小巷都传遍了。
t_en: What? Hold on... Are you from the Outside? Everyone's talking about your story!

t_ch: 哈哈，难怪你们不认识我。我刚才的态度多有冒犯，你们别介意啊！
t_en: Haha, no wonder you don't know me. That was embarrassing. Please excuse me!

t_ch: 我叫巴列维，是古代遗物鉴定的专家，这里认识我的人都叫我「矿灯」。幸会幸会。
t_en: My name is Balaway. I am an expert in relic appraisal. People all call me Miner's Lamp. Nice to meet you.

t_ch: 总之，本「矿灯」巴列维即日起停止一切鉴定服务——说来话长，我这有份公告，你拿去自己看吧。
t_en: Anyway, effective from today, I, "Miner's Lamp" Balaway will stop all appraisal services. It's a long story. You can have a look at this notice.

t_ch: 还是说你有别的什么事？
t_en: Or do you have other business?

t_ch: 我想和你聊聊…
t_en: Can we talk...?

t_ch: 古代遗物到底是什么？
t_en: What are relics?

t_ch: …你当真不是来拿我寻开心的？
t_en: ...Are you sure you're not here to mess with me?

t_ch: 算了算了，我理解。外来人嘛，不懂贝洛伯格的历史很正常。
t_en: Fine, fine. I understand. You are an Outsider. It's normal that you don't know the history of Belobog.

t_ch: 既然你都这么问了，那我就给你简单讲一讲——你知道「大寒潮」吧？
t_en: Now that you've asked, let me explain to you. Do you know the "Eternal Freeze?"

t_ch: 知道。
t_en: Yes.

t_ch: 不错，看来你多少了解过这里的历史。
t_en: Good. You seem to know some of the history here.

t_ch: 不知道。
t_en: I don't know.

t_ch: …你们真是打外边来的？真没骗我？
t_en: ...Are you really from the Outside? You are not lying?

t_ch: 虽然这城墙里面一年到头都暖和得很，但城墙外可是铺天盖地的暴风雪——如果你们真是从外面来的，想必是已经亲眼见过了。
t_en: Although it's warm inside the wall all year round, there are constant snowstorms outside the wall. If you are really from the Outside, you must have seen it.

t_ch: 这暴风雪可不是偶尔刮两阵就算完事…从七百年前到现在，这大雪就一分一秒也没停过。
t_en: This snowstorm isn't just temporary... Since seven hundred years ago, this heavy snow hasn't stopped for a minute.

t_ch: 而在暴风雪来临之前，贝洛伯格的科技水平要比现在高得多——贝洛伯格居民一般就把那段时期称呼为「古代」。
t_en: Before the snowstorm, Belobog's technology level was way much higher than it is now. Belobog residents call that "ancient times."

t_ch: 所以，顾名思义，「古代遗物」自然是大寒潮来临之前的遗留物了。
t_en: As the name suggests, relics are antiques from before the Eternal Freeze.

t_ch: 你为什么不搞鉴定了？
t_en: Why did you stop doing appraisals?

t_ch: 唉…说来话长，我这有份公告，你拿去自己看吧。
t_en: Well... It's a long story. This is the announcement. Have a look.

t_ch: 你都鉴定过什么东西？
t_en: What have you appraised?

t_ch: 哈！那可多了去了，两三天也说不完。
t_en: Ha! Too many! I can keep talking about it for two or three days.

t_ch: 不过啊，要说最有意思的，那当属旧世界的刑具。
t_en: But the most interesting thing is the torture device from the Old World.

t_ch: 比如什么…箍在脖子上的项圈，或者是能自动加热的铁砧？啧啧，旧世界罪犯的日子可真难过唷。
t_en: Like... collar around the neck or anvil that automatically heats up? Tsk tsk, the life of criminals in the Old World must be hard.

t_ch: 你的鉴定流程是怎么样的？
t_en: What is your appraisal process?

t_ch: 这个嘛…首先，肯定得要客人把古代遗物带到我这边来，对吧？
t_en: Well... First of all, people bring their relics to me, okay?

t_ch: 交到我手里之后呢，就轮到我进行鉴定了。鉴定过后，我会给出相关功能说明和价格参考。
t_en: After it is handed over to me, I will start to evaluate it. After the appraisal, I will tell them the relevant functions and a price reference.

t_ch: 基本上到这里就完事了，最后就是我根据鉴定难度收费的环节而已。
t_en: Basically, that's it. The last step is I'll charge according to the difficulty of appraisal.

t_ch: 我对你的鉴定方法有点兴趣。
t_en: I'm interested in your appraisal method.

t_ch: 怎么区分鉴定难度？
t_en: How do you distinguish the difficulty of the appraisal?

t_ch: 这个说起来可有点复杂。大部分尺寸比较小的物件结构也相对简单，只要简单参考些资料，那就能对它的功能构造略知一二了。
t_en: It's a bit complicated. Most smaller items have relatively simple structures. You can find out its function structure by referring to respective documents.

t_ch: 麻烦的是那些大件。一般来说，我得把他们彻底拆解，仔细检查一遍才行。
t_en: The bigger items are the real trouble. Generally speaking, I have to disassemble them and check everything carefully.

t_ch: 拆解？
t_en: Disassemble?

t_ch: 没错。很多遗物的功能和价值，只有完全拆解之后才能准确判断。
t_en: Right. The function and value of relics can only be appraised accurately after they are disassembled.

t_ch: 顺带一提，古代遗物拆解之后都有无法复原的风险。
t_en: By the way, once the relics are disassembled, there is a risk that they can't be reassembled.

t_ch: 而这个风险嘛…自然是要各位顾客自己承担咯。
t_en: And the risk... Naturally is to be borne by customers themselves.

t_ch: 参考资料？
t_en: Refer to documents?

t_ch: 旧世界留下的报纸和杂志残页，我全都给收集起来了。
t_en: I have collected all the remaining newspapers and magazines left from the Old World.

t_ch: 这些残页上刊登了模样和功能都比较类似的物品，一般还是很有参考价值的。
t_en: There are articles that show items with similar appearance and function. They are of a great reference value.

t_ch: 原来如此。
t_en: I see.

t_ch: 这古代遗物里的门道不少吧？今天就算是让你们这些外行开开眼了，哈哈！
t_en: There is a lot of knowledge about relics, right? It must be eye-opening for laymen like you, haha!

t_ch: 我没什么事了。
t_en: I don't have any other questions.

t_ch: 好吧，不能鉴定可真遗憾。
t_en: Well, it's a pity that you don't do appraisals anymore.

t_ch: 能有我遗憾吗？算了，不说了……
t_en: It's more of a pity for me. Forget it...

t_ch: 走开走开，我这边已经停止鉴定古代遗物的业务了……
t_en: Go away. The relic appraisal service is no longer offered...

t_ch: 不过你要是有别的什么事，不妨说来听听。
t_en: But you can tell me if you have some other business.

t_ch: 最近过得怎么样？
t_en: How have things been?

t_ch: 就那样呗，反正是不做鉴定古代遗物的行当了。
t_en: Same as always. Anyway, I'm not in the relic appraisal business anymore.

t_ch: 不过，我倒是在考虑要不要重启这项业务……
t_en: However, I've been thinking about getting back into it...

t_ch: 好啊，我支持你。
t_en: Alright, I've got your back.

t_ch: 哈哈，就冲你这句话——你要是找我做鉴定，我肯定给你八折优惠！
t_en: Haha, if that's the case, then I'll give you twenty percent off if you come see me for your appraisals!

t_ch: 要是磐岩镇的人都像你一样讲道理，我哪儿还犯得着暂停鉴定业务啊。还好马上就能去上层区了……
t_en: If the people of Boulder Town were more like you, I'd never have stopped my appraisal business. Fortunately, we'll be allowed up in the Overworld soon...

t_ch: 你怎么想一出是一出？
t_en: How can you be so impulsive?

t_ch: 嗐，这不是要顺应时代潮流嘛。你也听说了吧？上下层区估计最近就要重新开放往来了——
t_en: Hey, I'm just following the trend, right? Haven't you heard the same? They're opening up the Overworld and the Underworld again...

t_ch: 上层区的人可都是讲道理的人。哪像磐岩镇的这帮矿民，一言不合就要卸我胳膊腿……
t_en: They're all reasonable people up there in the Overworld. Not like these miners down here in Boulder Town who'd carve me up if I say the wrong thing...

t_ch: 既然没有生命危险了，那我为什么不重新开始鉴定古代遗物呢？回头我要是开张了，你也记得来捧个场啊，哈哈哈。
t_en: Now that my life's not in danger, why don't I start over with my relic appraisal business? Remember to stop by next time and give me some business, haha!

t_ch: 居然提前放工了…得去找点事情做。
t_en: Can't believe I finish work early today... I gotta find something to do.

t_ch: 听说洞螈肉最近涨价了，要是能抓到一两只，今天也算是没有浪费……
t_en: I heard olm meat has risen in price recently. It'd be a day well spent if I could catch a few...

t_ch: 提前放工了？
t_en: Early day off?

t_ch: 欸，我说出声了吗？抱歉……
t_en: Eh, did I say that aloud? Sorry...

t_ch: 是啊。今天收工比较早，大家都回去啦。
t_en: Yeah, we finish early today. Everyone's left.

t_ch: 抓洞螈？
t_en: Catch olms?

t_ch: 欸，我说出声了吗？抱歉……
t_en: Eh, did I say that aloud? Sorry...

t_ch: 是啊。今天收工早，所以我自己出来找点事做。
t_en: Yeah, we finish early today. I'm out on my own trying to look for something to do.

t_ch: 那个，我们之前有见过吗？抱歉，我好像没什么印象了…
t_en: Er, have we met before? Sorry, I don't seem to remember...

t_ch: 是我把你从大矿区救出来的…
t_en: I saved you from the Great Mine...

t_ch: 啊！我想起来了！抱歉，我的视力很差，居然连自己的救命恩人都没看清楚！
t_en: Ahhhh! I remember now! Sorry, I have poor vision. Can't believe I didn't recognize the person who saved my life!

t_ch: 我们是刚从矿坑里出土的…
t_en: We were just unearthed from a mine...

t_ch: 欸！矿坑里挖出了大活人？我可没听说有这种事啊…你不介意让我贴近点看看吧？
t_en: What? Living humans from the mine? I've never heard of that... Do you mind if I take a closer look?

t_ch: 哎呀，原来是你！抱歉，我的视力太差了，居然连自己的救命恩人都没看清楚！
t_en: What? It's you! I'm sorry I have poor vision. Can't believe I didn't recognize the person who saved my life!

t_ch: 之前在大矿区能获得你的帮助，我真是太幸运了…要不然的话，我的全部积蓄都可能被那群流浪者给抢走了……
t_en: I was so lucky to be saved by you in the Great Mine... Or my entire savings would've been taken away by those tramps...

t_ch: 那个，现在暂时没看到有洞螈活动的迹象…请问你有什么想聊的吗？
t_en: Well, I don't think there are signs of olm activities right now... is there anything else you want to chat about?

t_ch: 那来聊天吧…
t_en: Let's chat...

t_ch: 地下有什么值得去的地方吗？
t_en: Any place worth visiting underground?

t_ch: 值得去的地方吗？我想想……
t_en: Worth visiting? Let me think...

t_ch: 歌德大饭店应该算一个吧？然后好像就剩搏击俱乐部了。
t_en: The Goethe Grand Hotel probably. And then it's the Fight Club.

t_ch: 讲讲歌德大饭店吧。
t_en: Tell me about the Goethe Grand Hotel.

t_ch: 歌德大饭店就在那边，喏。怎么样，很气派吧？毕竟这是歌德家族在下层区的头家店铺呢，而且还是贝洛伯格的总店哦！
t_en: The Goethe Grand Hotel is right there. It's fancy, isn't it? After all, this is the Goethe family's first shop in the Underworld. It's the main store in Belobog!

t_ch: 饭店的主人是歌蒂大婶，也是我师傅，不知道你们见过没…
t_en: Gertie is the owner of the hotel and also my master. Not sure if you've met her...

t_ch: 她的厨艺很棒的，你们有机会一定要尝一尝。
t_en: She's a great cook. You should try the food she makes.

t_ch: 讲讲搏击俱乐部吧。
t_en: Tell me about the Fight Club.

t_ch: 搏击俱乐部…据说每天都有职业拳手的搏击表演，甚至还有怪物什么的…晚上总是很热闹。
t_en: The Fight Club... it's said that there are professional boxers fighting and monsters everyday... it's especially lively at night.

t_ch: 不过我不喜欢看那些，有点害怕，就…其实我也没去过。这些都是我道听途说的，不好意思呀。
t_en: But I don't like those. They scare me... I've never been there. I only heard this from other people. I'm sorry.

t_ch: 原来如此，谢谢你。
t_en: I see. Thank you.

t_ch: 没事没事！虽然这地下不像上层区那么气派，但也希望你们能在这儿玩得开心！
t_en: No worries! Although the Underworld is not as fancy as the Overworld, I hope you'll enjoy your time here!

t_ch: 你为什么想抓洞螈？
t_en: Why do you want to catch olms?

t_ch: 嗯…因为洞螈很好吃？
t_en: Hmm... because they are delicious?

t_ch: 虽然这个答案听起来像玩笑，但事实就是这样哦。洞螈在这下层区可是经常上餐桌的呢，很多人都会买来吃的。
t_en: It may sound funny but it's true. Olms are a common food in the Underworld. Many people would buy them for cooking.

t_ch: 说到这个——你们要是有机会去歌德大饭店的话，可一定要尝尝他们家的铁罐焖螈肉。
t_en: Speaking of which, make sure you try the Iron Pot Braised Olm at the Goethe Grand Hotel.

t_ch: 我敢和你们打包票：这是下层区最美味的洞螈料理，没有之一！
t_en: I can assure you, that is the most delicious olm dish in the Underworld!

t_ch: 啊…一想到那香酥的面包皮和多汁鲜嫩的洞螈肉，我的口水都要流下来啦。真不愧是歌蒂师傅啊……
t_en: Ahhh... it makes me drool whenever I think of the crispy puff and the juicy olm meat. Master Gertie is such a wonderful cook...

t_ch: 你平时都会挣外快吗？
t_en: Do you take any side jobs?

t_ch: 唔…基本上是吧？平时放工之后我就会拿着捕网去捉洞螈……
t_en: Well... I do. I will grab a net to catch olms after work...

t_ch: 不过，总感觉挣不到什么钱呢。我经常是好不容易捉到两三只，想要拿去卖掉的时候，就被告知洞螈已经降价了……
t_en: However, I don't think I'm making any money. I usually can only catch two or three. When I try to sell them, I am told the price of olm has dropped...

t_ch: 你试过捉洞螈以外的兼职吗？
t_en: Have you ever tried any other part-time job than catching olms?

t_ch: 嗯…是有尝试过啦，还不少呢。
t_en: Well... I did, actually quite a few.

t_ch: 不过也就仅限于尝试啦…毕竟我到最后总会发现，其他活计做起来比捉洞螈累姑且不说，挣得也没有这么多……
t_en: But I only tried once or twice... In the end I realize the other jobs are more tiring than catching olms. Also, I'm not making much more money...

t_ch: 可是捉洞螈也赚不了多少嘛！真搞不懂为什么会这样…是我运气太差了吗？呜呜……
t_en: But catching olms is already not very profitable! I really don't get it... am I just unlucky? *sob*

t_ch: 没事了，你加油。
t_en: I shouldn't keep you. Best of luck.

t_ch: 好像有什么东西钻出来了…
t_en: Something's coming out...

t_ch: 欸？洞螈钻出来了吗？
t_en: Hey? Did the olm come out?

t_ch: 不好意思，我得去拿捕网了…先失陪啦。
t_en: Sorry, I have to get my net... Excuse me.

t_ch: 唉，今天还一条洞螈都没抓到呢…是我太着急了吗？
t_en: *sigh* I haven't caught any olms today... Is it because I'm not patient enough?

t_ch: …咦？你是什么时候来的？不好意思呀，我在抓洞螈呢，没注意到你。
t_en: ...Hey? When did you arrive? Sorry, I was catching olms and I didn't notice you were here.

t_ch: 不过估计一时半会也抓不到洞螈啦…要先来聊聊天吗？
t_en: But I don't think I can catch any olms any time soon... Do you want to chat with me?

t_ch: 最近过得怎么样？
t_en: How have things been?

t_ch: 最近？我正打算去上层区找点兼职呢，嘿嘿。
t_en: Well, I plan to look for some part-time jobs in the Overworld, hehe.

t_ch: 不抓洞螈啦？
t_en: No more catching olms?

t_ch: 是啊…忙活了这么久，抓洞螈赚的钱还不够我去集会所买杯饮料呢。不过我的洞螈烹饪手艺倒是有进步……
t_en: Yes... I can't even afford a drink at the Gathering Place with what I've earned from catching olms. Still, I've become a better cook of olm dishes...

t_ch: 那你矿场的工作怎么办？
t_en: What about your job at the mine?
